idea.title: Tidepool
idea.hook: Eat to grow, but the exit gates only fit a precise size — gorge too much and you're stuck, so let a predator nip you to shrink back down.
idea.hook_ru: Ешь и расти, но выходные ворота открываются только под точный размер — переешь и застрянешь, так что дай хищнику куснуть тебя, чтобы уменьшиться.
idea.prototype: You are a small round creature in a sunlit tidepool. Swim by dragging/pointing (one-finger steer-toward-point) with an optional DASH burst. Eat glowing bits SMALLER than you to grow your size meter. The level EXIT gate only opens for a creature inside a precise size window (target +/- tolerance), so size management is the puzzle: grow to fit, and if you OVERSHOOT, brush a bigger predator on purpose — it NIPS you and shrinks you back down (it only SWALLOWS you when you're small, costing a life). 8 levels with rising gate-size precision (tolerance tightens), multiple gates in sequence requiring grow->shrink->grow re-sizing, and predators introduced as both the shrink tool and the lives threat. A live size meter marks the target window so the goal reads in seconds. Non-timer pressure: size-window scarcity (limited bits per level) + 3 lives.
idea.why_novel: An eat-and-grow agar-style loop inverted into a PRECISION puzzle: growth is not a monotonic win — overshooting the gate window is a fail state, and the only shrink mechanic is the genre's usual death threat (a bigger predator) repurposed as a deliberate tool. Nothing in the portfolio uses 'size as a two-sided resource you can overshoot' or 'brush the predator to deflate' as the core puzzle. The daily-mix tagged this vehicle_crash, but the mechanic is eat-and-grow, so the build borrows the weird_creature/cozy_cute portal pattern (cute round creature mid-gulp) for CTR while shipping a genuine size-fit puzzle.
idea.non_timer_pressure: size-window scarcity + lives. Edible bits are a bounded budget per level (bitsRemaining), so over-eating past the gate window is a real risk with no easy undo except brushing a predator (which risks a life). MAX_LIVES=3: a predator swallowing you while small (size < predator.r*SWALLOW_RATIO=0.5) costs a life; a STRAND_LIMIT=9s soft-lock guard prevents dead-ends. No countdown timer drives the lose condition.
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when lives<=0. Lives are lost when a predator swallows you while you're too small to be safe (size < predator.r*0.5), or via the strand guard (no bits left, wrong size, no predator) after STRAND_LIMIT seconds.

synthesizer_brief: This is a market-SIGNAL transfer_bet pick from the 2026-05-26 opportunities daily-mix (#4 Tidepool), chosen as the strongest mobile-transfer bet (mobile score 81.9) with verdict build_now. The radar tagged the racing_vehicle / vehicle_crash market family, but the underlying idea is eat-and-grow, mirrored by the nearby Yandex/CrazyGames signal 'Eat & Grow Fish'. Rather than force a vehicle-crash cover, the build follows the CTR note to borrow the weird_creature/cozy_cute portal pattern: a chubby round creature mid-gulp, big and clickable, swelling toward a too-small glowing gate (CTR refs: Eat & Grow Fish on crazygames; Cattosu Chonk Cat Merge, a cozy_cute weird_creature card). The novelty over a plain agar clone is making SIZE a precision target with a two-sided fail (too big OR too small) and turning the predator from pure threat into the shrink tool. Clarity-first per portfolio comment data: level 1 shows the player's size, the gate's required window (green band on the meter), and a few edible bits, so 'eat to grow, fit the gate' is learned by doing. Real depth: 8 levels, tightening tolerance, multi-gate sequences, predators as shrink tool + lives pressure.

sources.snippet: #4 Tidepool — market family racing_vehicle, verdict build_now, transfer_bet, mobile 81.9 (strongest mobile-transfer bet). Hook: eat to grow but the exit gates only fit a precise size; gorge too much and you're stuck, so let a predator nip you to shrink back down.

Nearby market signal 'Eat & Grow Fish' (crazygames.com/game/eat-and-grow-fish) — the eat-and-grow fish loop the Tidepool puzzle mutates from. CTR ref also: Cattosu Chonk Cat Merge (crazygames, cozy_cute weird_creature).

alternatives.title: Leak Run
alternatives.hook: Patch bursting pipes in sequence as pressure rises — plug the biggest leak first or the whole line floods.
alternatives.why_novel: Rising-pressure triage puzzle (resource = patches, not time); passed over because the leak-prioritisation read is less instantly obvious than Tidepool's size-bar goal.

alternatives.title: Reagent
alternatives.hook: Mix two-color reagents to hit a precise target shade — overshoot the hue and you must dilute back.
alternatives.why_novel: Same 'overshoot a precise target' DNA as Tidepool but on a color axis; Tidepool won for the cuter, more clickable cozy_cute creature cover and clearer spatial gameplay.

alternatives.title: Limelight
alternatives.hook: Keep a nervous performer inside a roaming spotlight as the beam shrinks and wanders across the stage.
alternatives.why_novel: Shrinking-safe-zone positional pressure; held back because it overlaps the dodger/positioning space more than the fresh size-resource puzzle Tidepool offers (and is already slotted as 104_limelight).

alternatives.title: Heave
alternatives.hook: Time rhythmic heaves to haul a heavy net up a cliff — pull on the upswing, rest on the downswing or you slip.
alternatives.why_novel: Rhythm/momentum tug mechanic with slip-back pressure; passed over because the timing window is harder to telegraph clearly on a static cover than Tidepool's mid-gulp creature.

alternatives.title: Flailaway
alternatives.hook: Swing a flailing rag-doll limb to knock targets in the right order using only physics momentum.
alternatives.why_novel: Physics-flail comedy with momentum-only control; fun but the control read is fiddly on mobile portrait compared to Tidepool's one-finger swim, so deprioritised for this slot.
